; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|12=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|12=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as a Shikinaisha of Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|12=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|12=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

revision.text:
'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|12=draft|12=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} as a Shikinaisha of Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|12=draft|12=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}} as a Shikinaisha of Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha of Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

revision.text:
'''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、日本各地にある[[神社]]。
* [[高野神社 (山武市)]] - [[千葉県]][[山武市]]鎮座。 * [[高野神社 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]鎮座。 * [[高野神社 (かつらぎ町)]] - [[和歌山県]][[伊都郡]][[かつらぎ町]]鎮座。 * [[高野神社 (津山市二宮)]] - [[岡山県]][[津山市]]二宮鎮座。[[式内社]]、[[美作国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|二宮]]。 * [[高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)]] - 岡山県津山市高野本郷鎮座。式内社。 * [[高野神社 (松山市)]] - [[愛媛県]][[松山市]]鎮座。 * [[高野神社 (多久市)]] - [[佐賀県]][[多久市]]鎮座。
{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha of Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

<!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine--><!--Takano Shrine-->

'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 高野神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Ninomiya Takano jinja 06.jpg|280px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[津山市]]二宮601 |位置 = {{Coord|35|03|33.43|N|133|57|52.03|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高野神社}} |祭神 = [[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安閑天皇]]2年 |本殿 = [[中山造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 美作三大社 |例祭 = [[10月25日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama Prefecture|label=高野神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]、[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 概要 == [[津山市]]西部に位置し、北西に神山を背負い、南に久米の佐良山を望み、眼下に[[吉井川]]を見下ろす地に鎮座する。 [[美作国]]の[[一宮|二宮]]とされた古社であり、美作三大社の一社に数えられる美作三大社の他の二社は、[[中山神社]]と[[美作総社宮]]。。中世以降、領主や[[津山藩|津山藩主]]の崇敬が篤く、なかでも藩主・[[森氏]]が社殿の造営・社領の寄進を行い、当時の本殿は岡山県指定重要文化財となっている。そのほか、国の重要文化財の随身像などの文化財を現在に伝えている。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊]]''' （ひこなぎさたけうがやふきあえずのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[大己貴命]] （おおなむちのみこと） - [[美作総社宮]]祭神 * 鏡作命 （かがみつくりのみこと） - [[中山神社]]（美作国[[一宮]]）祭神 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝では、当社の祭祀は吉井川の河原にあった碫馭盧岩（おのころいわ）に始まり、この岩を[[磐境]]として祀っていたと伝える[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html 高野神社 由緒]（公式サイト）。。のち[[安閑天皇]]2年（[[534年]]一般的に安閑天皇2年は[[535年]]とされる。「[[安閑天皇]]」も参照。）に社殿が創建されたという[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁）。。 === 概史 === [[平安時代]]中期の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[式内社]]として「美作国[[苫東郡]] 高野神社」の記載があり、当社をそれにあてる説がある。当社は遅くとも平安時代後期から美作国[[一宮|二宮]]として栄えていたとされ、当社を比定する説は多い。ただし当社の鎮座地は[[苫西郡]]であるため、苫東郡に鎮座する[[高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|高野神社]]（津山市高野本郷）をあてる説もあり、両方が[[論社]]とされ結論には至っていない。なお『延喜式神名帳』国史大系本には「カウヤ」と仮名が振られているほか、『[[宇治拾遺物語]]』には当社に関して「かうや」と仮名で記されており、社名は一時期音読されていたことがわかる。 [[鎌倉時代]]以降は武家からの崇敬も篤く、[[源頼朝]]・[[山名時春]]・[[毛利元就]]・[[小早川隆景]]等が社殿造営や寄進を行なったと伝える。[[江戸時代]]には[[津山藩|津山藩主]]・森氏により現在の社殿が修造されたほか、のちの藩主となった[[越前松平家|松平氏]]からも崇敬された。 [[明治維新]]後、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列した。 === 神階 === 「美作国高野神」に対する奉叙の記録。ただし、当社とするか高野本郷の[[高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|高野神社]]とするかには検討を要する『岡山県の地名』高野神社項。。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）8月15日、従五位上 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） * 貞観17年（[[875年]]）3月29日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） == 境内 == === 社殿 === [[File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 08.jpg|thumb|200px|left|本殿]] * 本殿（県指定重要文化財） : 江戸時代の[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）、津山藩2代藩主・[[森長継]]による再建。 : この地方独特の[[中山造]]で、正面三間・側面四間・一重入母屋造・妻入、向拝一間・向唐破風造、銅板葺である{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5230,c,html/20070124-163748.pdf 津山市の県指定文化財2]}}。。 {{-}} File:Takano-jinja (tsuyamashi-ninomiya) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 09.jpg|宝物殿
重要文化財指定品等の宝物を納める。 File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 02.jpg|随身門（市指定重要文化財）
[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）の再建という。覆屋は[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）に付設境内説明板。。 === 宇那提森 === [[File:Takano-jinja (tsuyamashi-ninomiya) mukunoki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|宇那提森のムクノキ（市指定天然記念物）]] '''宇那提森'''（うなでのもり、うなでがもり）は、当社鎮座地の森。『五大集歌枕』や『[[八雲御抄]]』で美作国の[[歌枕]]として記載されている『岡山県の地名』二宮村項。。 『[[万葉集]]』には「卯名手(うなて)の社」の記載があり「真鳥住む 卯名手の神社(もり)の 菅の根を 衣に書付け 服せむ児もがも」作者未詳（『万葉集』1344番）。思はぬを 思ふといはば 真鳥住む 卯名手の社の 神し知らさむ - 作者未詳（『万葉集』3100番）。、これは[[大和国]][[高市郡]]雲梯郷（現 [[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）にあてるのが通説だが、後世の歌学書はこの「うなてのもり」を用水の神の普通名詞として美作国にあてている。また、美作国の「うなての社」で雨乞いをする様子を記す文献もあり『経信卿母集』後記に記載（『岡山県の地名』二宮村項より）。、水にまつわる神としての当社の信仰がうかがわれる。 伝承によると、この森には大蛇が棲んでおり、人畜に害をもたらし神社の扁額を舐めまわしたと言われる。当社所蔵文化財のうち[[藤原行成]]筆と伝える扁額「正一位高野大明神」が残っている。大蛇は森で殺されたといい、現在も残る蛇塚・胴塚に埋められたという。 宇那提森は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に砦構築のため[[宇喜多氏]]により伐採され、現在は石鳥居付近に一本のムクノキのみが残され市の天然記念物に指定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 八幡神社 * 籠神社 * 淡島神社 * 御先神社 * 荒神社 * 広瀬神社 * 漆神社 == 主な祭事 == * 歳旦祭 （[[1月1日]]） * 夏祭 （[[7月18日]]） * 秋祭 （[[10月22日]]に近い日曜日） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 木造神号額 : [[明治]]34年（[[1901年]]）8月2日指定。 * 木造随身立像 2躯 応保二年（1162年）筑後講師厳成の銘あり : [[平安時代]]の作。[[昭和]]31年（[[1956年]]）6月28日指定。 * 木造獅子 2対 : 平安時代。一対は10世紀頃、もう一対は12世紀頃の作。[[狛犬]]のように見えるが、頭上に角をもたない「獅子」像で、この種の作例としては、奈良・[[薬師寺]]像とともに日本最古に属する。[[平成]]16年（[[2004年]]）6月8日指定。「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』489号（第一法規、2004）、pp.24 - 25 === 岡山県指定重要文化財 === * 本殿 - 昭和58年（[[1983年]]）4月8日指定 === 津山市指定重要文化財 === * 随身門及び覆屋 - 平成11年（[[1999年]]）9月24日指定 === 津山市指定天然記念物 === * 宇那提森のムクノキ - 昭和48年（[[1973年]]）10月20日指定 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[津山市]]二宮601 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[姫新線]] [[院庄駅]] （徒歩約10分） ; 周辺 * 美和山古墳群 - 国の史跡。美作地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]を有する * [[作楽神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 『美作二宮 式内 高野神社略誌』（高野神社社務所） * 『日本歴史地名体系 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）津山市 二宮村項、高野神社項 * 岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会 編『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、1991年）198ページ * 谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 2 山陽 四国』（[[白水社]]）高野神社項 ==外部リンク == {{commonscat|Takano-jinja (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html |title=高野神社 |date=20110404000858}}（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html#ﾓｸｿﾞｳｼﾝｺﾞｳｶﾞｸ 木造神号額]、[http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html#ﾓｸｿﾞｳﾘｭｳｼﾝ 木造随身立像2躯]、[http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html#ﾓｸｿﾞｳｼｼ12 木造獅子2対]（津山市ホームページ） * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやましにのみや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の重要文化財]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧県社]] [[Category:美作国|社2]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]]

社殿 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[津山市]]二宮601 |位置 = {{Coord|35|03|33.43|N|133|57|52.03|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高野神社}} |祭神 = [[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安閑天皇]]2年 |本殿 = [[中山造]] |別名 = |札所等 = 美作三大社 |例祭 = [[10月25日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama Prefecture|label=高野神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 01.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''高野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[津山市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]、[[美作国]][[一宮|二宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 概要 == [[津山市]]西部に位置し、北西に神山を背負い、南に久米の佐良山を望み、眼下に[[吉井川]]を見下ろす地に鎮座する。 [[美作国]]の[[一宮|二宮]]とされた古社であり、美作三大社の一社に数えられる美作三大社の他の二社は、[[中山神社]]と[[美作総社宮]]。。中世以降、領主や[[津山藩|津山藩主]]の崇敬が篤く、なかでも藩主・[[森氏]]が社殿の造営・社領の寄進を行い、当時の本殿は岡山県指定重要文化財となっている。そのほか、国の重要文化財の随身像などの文化財を現在に伝えている。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[ウガヤフキアエズ|彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊]]''' （ひこなぎさたけうがやふきあえずのみこと） ; 相殿神 * [[大己貴命]] （おおなむちのみこと） - [[美作総社宮]]祭神 * 鏡作命 （かがみつくりのみこと） - [[中山神社]]（美作国[[一宮]]）祭神 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝では、当社の祭祀は吉井川の河原にあった碫馭盧岩（おのころいわ）に始まり、この岩を[[磐境]]として祀っていたと伝える[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html 高野神社 由緒]（公式サイト）。。のち[[安閑天皇]]2年（[[534年]]一般的に安閑天皇2年は[[535年]]とされる。「[[安閑天皇]]」も参照。）に社殿が創建されたという[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁）。。 === 概史 === [[平安時代]]中期の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[式内社]]として「美作国[[苫東郡]] 高野神社」の記載があり、当社をそれにあてる説がある。当社は遅くとも平安時代後期から美作国[[一宮|二宮]]として栄えていたとされ、当社を比定する説は多い。ただし当社の鎮座地は[[苫西郡]]であるため、苫東郡に鎮座する[[高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|高野神社]]（津山市高野本郷）をあてる説もあり、両方が[[論社]]とされ結論には至っていない。なお『延喜式神名帳』国史大系本には「カウヤ」と仮名が振られているほか、『[[宇治拾遺物語]]』には当社に関して「かうや」と仮名で記されており、社名は一時期音読されていたことがわかる。 [[鎌倉時代]]以降は武家からの崇敬も篤く、[[源頼朝]]・[[山名時春]]・[[毛利元就]]・[[小早川隆景]]等が社殿造営や寄進を行なったと伝える。[[江戸時代]]には[[津山藩|津山藩主]]・森氏により現在の社殿が修造されたほか、のちの藩主となった[[越前松平家|松平氏]]からも崇敬された。 [[明治維新]]後、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列した。 === 神階 === 「美作国高野神」に対する奉叙の記録。ただし、当社とするか高野本郷の[[高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|高野神社]]とするかには検討を要する『岡山県の地名』高野神社項。。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）8月15日、従五位上 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） * 貞観17年（[[875年]]）3月29日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） == 境内 == === 社殿 === [[File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 08.jpg|thumb|200px|left|本殿]] * 本殿（県指定重要文化財） : 江戸時代の[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）、津山藩2代藩主・[[森長継]]による再建。 : この地方独特の[[中山造]]で、正面三間・側面四間・一重入母屋造・妻入、向拝一間・向唐破風造、銅板葺である{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5230,c,html/20070124-163748.pdf 津山市の県指定文化財2]}}。。 {{-}} File:Takano-jinja (tsuyamashi-ninomiya) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 09.jpg|宝物殿
重要文化財指定品等の宝物を納める。 File:Ninomiya Takano jinja 02.jpg|随身門（市指定重要文化財）
[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）の再建という。覆屋は[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）に付設境内説明板。。 === 宇那提森 === [[File:Takano-jinja (tsuyamashi-ninomiya) mukunoki.JPG|thumb|200px|right|宇那提森のムクノキ（市指定天然記念物）]] '''宇那提森'''（うなでのもり、うなでがもり）は、当社鎮座地の森。『五大集歌枕』や『[[八雲御抄]]』で美作国の[[歌枕]]として記載されている『岡山県の地名』二宮村項。。 『[[万葉集]]』には「卯名手(うなて)の社」の記載があり「真鳥住む 卯名手の神社(もり)の 菅の根を 衣に書付け 服せむ児もがも」作者未詳（『万葉集』1344番）。思はぬを 思ふといはば 真鳥住む 卯名手の社の 神し知らさむ - 作者未詳（『万葉集』3100番）。、これは[[大和国]][[高市郡]]雲梯郷（現 [[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）にあてるのが通説だが、後世の歌学書はこの「うなてのもり」を用水の神の普通名詞として美作国にあてている。また、美作国の「うなての社」で雨乞いをする様子を記す文献もあり『経信卿母集』後記に記載（『岡山県の地名』二宮村項より）。、水にまつわる神としての当社の信仰がうかがわれる。 伝承によると、この森には大蛇が棲んでおり、人畜に害をもたらし神社の扁額を舐めまわしたと言われる。当社所蔵文化財のうち[[藤原行成]]筆と伝える扁額「正一位高野大明神」が残っている。大蛇は森で殺されたといい、現在も残る蛇塚・胴塚に埋められたという。 宇那提森は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に砦構築のため[[宇喜多氏]]により伐採され、現在は石鳥居付近に一本のムクノキのみが残され市の天然記念物に指定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 八幡神社 * 籠神社 * 淡島神社 * 御先神社 * 荒神社 * 広瀬神社 * 漆神社 == 主な祭事 == * 歳旦祭 （[[1月1日]]） * 夏祭 （[[7月18日]]） * 秋祭 （[[10月22日]]に近い日曜日） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 木造神号額 : [[明治]]34年（[[1901年]]）8月2日指定。 * 木造随身立像 2躯 応保二年（1162年）筑後講師厳成の銘あり : [[平安時代]]の作。[[昭和]]31年（[[1956年]]）6月28日指定。 * 木造獅子 2対 : 平安時代。一対は10世紀頃、もう一対は12世紀頃の作。[[狛犬]]のように見えるが、頭上に角をもたない「獅子」像で、この種の作例としては、奈良・[[薬師寺]]像とともに日本最古に属する。[[平成]]16年（[[2004年]]）6月8日指定。「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』489号（第一法規、2004）、pp.24 - 25 === 岡山県指定重要文化財 === * 本殿 - 昭和58年（[[1983年]]）4月8日指定 === 津山市指定重要文化財 === * 随身門及び覆屋 - 平成11年（[[1999年]]）9月24日指定 === 津山市指定天然記念物 === * 宇那提森のムクノキ - 昭和48年（[[1973年]]）10月20日指定 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[津山市]]二宮601 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[姫新線]] [[院庄駅]] （徒歩約10分） ; 周辺 * 美和山古墳群 - 国の史跡。美作地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]を有する * [[作楽神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 『美作二宮 式内 高野神社略誌』（高野神社社務所） * 『日本歴史地名体系 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）津山市 二宮村項、高野神社項 * 岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会 編『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、1991年）198ページ * 谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 2 山陽 四国』（[[白水社]]）高野神社項 ==外部リンク == {{commonscat|Takano-jinja (Ninomiya, Tsuyama)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html |title=高野神社 |date=20110404000858}}（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html#ﾓｸｿﾞｳｼﾝｺﾞｳｶﾞｸ 木造神号額]、[http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html#ﾓｸｿﾞｳﾘｭｳｼﾝ 木造随身立像2躯]、[http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html#ﾓｸｿﾞｳｼｼ12 木造獅子2対]（津山市ホームページ） * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 高野神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしやつやましにのみや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:津山市の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の重要文化財]] [[Category:美作国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧県社]] [[Category:美作国|社2]] [[Category:津山市の建築物]]

'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).

The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. ; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.

=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''

'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>

Both remain disputed.

In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.

=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former Ken-sha shrines" to "Former Ken-sha"
revision.text:
'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''

== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/top/menu.html Takano Shrine Official Website (archived)] * [http://www.city.tsuyama.lg.jp/index.cfm/22,5208,116,371,html Overview of Takano Shrine Cultural Properties (Tsuyama City Website)] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=03005 Takano Shrine (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association)]

revision.text:
'''Takano Shrine''' (高野神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama City|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), a disputed {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimasaka Province|qq=}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a [[Ken-sha]] (prefectural shrine).
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated in the western part of Tsuyama City, with the sacred Mount Kami to the northwest and the Sarayama hills of Kume to the south, overlooking the Yoshii River. It is one of the three major shrines of Mimasaka Province, alongside {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimasaka Sōjagū|qq=}}.<ref>The other two major shrines of Mimasaka are Nakayama Shrine and Mimasaka Sōja Shrine.</ref>
Since the medieval period, the shrine enjoyed reverence from local lords, including the daimyō of the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Domain|qq=}}, the Mori family. The current main shrine, reconstructed by the Mori clan during the Edo period, is a designated Important Cultural Property of Okayama Prefecture. The shrine also houses national-level Important Cultural Properties, such as statues of Zuishin (guardian deities).
== Deities ==
; Primary deity * '''Hiko Nagisa Take Uga Fuki Aezunomikoto''' (彦波限建鵜葺草葺不合尊) – The main enshrined deity.
; Associated deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} – Deity of {{ill|Mimasaka Sōjagū|ja|美作総社宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimasaka Sōjagū|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|ja|鏡作命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Tsukuri no Mikoto|qq=}} – Deity of {{ill|Nakayama Shrine|ja|中山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Shrine|qq=}}, the Ichinomiya of Mimasaka Province.
== History ==
=== Foundation === According to shrine tradition, worship began at the Onokoro Iwa (碫馭盧岩) on the Yoshii River, which was regarded as a sacred rock (iwakura). The shrine itself was established during the second year of Emperor Ankan’s reign (534 CE) and subsequently moved to its current location.<ref name="origin">[http://space.geocities.jp/ninomiya_takano/contents/rekishi/yuisho/yuisho.html Takano Shrine History] (official site).</ref>
=== Development === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province. Some theories identify the shrine in question as Takano Shrine in Ninomiya, while others propose the {{ill|Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Shrine (Takahon-gō)|qq=}} in Takahon-gō. Both remain disputed.
The shrine gained prominence in the Kamakura period, receiving patronage from notable figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamana Tokiharu|ja|山名時春|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana Tokiharu|qq=}}, {{ill|Mōri Motonari|ja|毛利元就|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Motonari|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kobayakawa Takakage|ja|小早川隆景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayakawa Takakage|qq=}}. In the Edo period, the Mori family, daimyō of the Tsuyama Domain, rebuilt the shrine. Later, the Matsudaira clan, successors to the domain, also supported the shrine.
During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was classified as a [[Ken-sha]] under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Wooden Inscription Plaque''' – Designated August 2, 1901. * '''Wooden Zuishin Statues (2 figures)''' – Crafted in 1162. Designated June 28, 1956. * '''Wooden Lion Statues (2 pairs)''' – Dated to the 10th and 12th centuries. Considered among the oldest examples of this type in Japan. Designated June 8, 2004.<ref>“Newly Designated Cultural Properties,” *Monthly Cultural Properties*, Issue 489 (2004), pp. 24–25.</ref>
=== Prefectural and Municipal Designations === * '''Main Shrine''' – Prefectural designation in 1983. * '''Zuishin Gate and Roof Covering''' – Municipal designation in 1999. * '''Mukuno Tree in Unate Forest''' – Municipal designation in 1973.
== Festivals ==
* '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Summer Festival''' (July 18) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Sunday closest to October 22)
== Access == ; Address * 601 Ninomiya, Tsuyama City, Okayama Prefecture
; Transportation * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Innosho Station|ja|院庄駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Innosho Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} {{ill|Kishin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Line|qq=}}.
== Surrounding Area == * '''Miwayama Kofuns''' – The largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Mimasaka. * '''Sakaku Shrine'''
